title: BTC short position in +4,000 points
author: Spirited_Syllabub488
selftext:
https://preview.redd.it/ir4ru61wjt3h1.jpg?width=1272&format=pjpg&auto=webp&s=513a8759fa1a5fe566668806ea8460c50b8446b5

# 

BBTC short trade from **26-05-2026 UTC open** at **77,080**, after entering at **77,080** price went UP for some time but eventually came straight down after some huge volatility.

That makes it roughly **+4,000 points** on the trade. Around **8% of account** in a single trade.

What I liked about this one is that it was a good example of trusting the system through noise. The position went against me at one point, but the setup stayed valid, so I let it play out instead of forcing an early exit. In the end, that patience paid off.



I’ve been live trading the system since September, 2025, and trades like this are a big part of why I believe in its process. The system is designed to adapt, stay disciplined, and capture the move when the market eventually confirms it.

**6 years of backtest of my model with fixed risk exposure shows:**

* CAGR \~42%
* SHARPE >3
* Average Monthly ROI \~10%
* Max DD \~22%

Report: [https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Y7YEhn8JnWKGg47\_DojXwfRj7cAWo2dI/view?usp=drive\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Y7YEhn8JnWKGg47_DojXwfRj7cAWo2dI/view?usp=drive_link)

Open for discussion and acquisition.

comments.author: Impossible-Band-2393
comments.body: Just worth remembering that live results can still diverge from backtests due to regime shifts and execution, so ongoing validation and risk caps matter as much as edge.

replies.author: Spirited_Syllabub488
replies.body:
My system is ML based, that it means the model retrains periodically with newer data and that keeps my model updated with market conditions.

But I am looking to sell it due to capital limitations. 

Would you be interested in it mate?

comments.author: Aggravating_Swan_436
comments.body: The long-term stats also suggest the system has solid consistency if those results hold in live conditions.

comments.author: Choice_Run1329
comments.body:
Trusting the system through drawdown is where most algo traders blow up, so that discipline is real. One thing I'd push back on though, a Sharpe >3 over 6 years of backtest almost always degrades live, especially in crypto where regime shifts eat mean-reversion models alive. How's slippage modeled?

That 22% max DD feels optimistic too. I forward-tested a BTC short system on markets xyz for about three months and the overnight weekend gaps alone added ~4% to realized drawdown versus backtest.

replies.author: Spirited_Syllabub488
replies.body:
My system is ML based, the system is designed in a way so that the system retrains itself on newer data and that method allows my model to adapt itself on newer market data.

My system is live since September, 2025 and it has shown very good results around 70,000 points of gain when BTC is down by around 40,000 points in this period. That data is also mentioned in the report.
